parameters.name: order_by.sort_field
parameters.description:
The field to order by

 - SORT_FIELD_UNSPECIFIED: The sort field is unspecified
 - SORT_FIELD_NAME: Sort the folder by name
 - SORT_FIELD_FOLDER_ID: Sort the folders by folder id
 - SORT_FIELD_USER_ID: Sort the folders by user id
 - SORT_FIELD_CREATED_AT: Sort the folders by created at
 - SORT_FIELD_MODIFIED_AT: Sort the folders by modified at

parameters.name: order_by.sort_order
parameters.description:
The direction to order by

 - SORT_ORDER_UNSPECIFIED: The sort order is not specified
 - SORT_ORDER_ASCENDING: The sort order is asc
 - SORT_ORDER_DESCENDING: The sort order is desc

parameters.name: only_leaf_nodes
parameters.description:
Optional. When true, GetFolders will only return leaf node folders. leaf
nodes are folders that do not have children  Defaults to false.

parameters.name: with_ancestors
parameters.description:
Optional. When true, folders will be returned with a list of their
ancestors as well. Defaults to false

parameters.name: include_deleted
parameters.description: When true, deleted folders will also be returned

parameters.name: ignore_folders_disabled
parameters.description:
Optional. When true, folders will still be returned when disabled for the
organisation

parameters.name: domain
parameters.description:
domain represents the type/domain/hierarchy for the folder. This is used
for getting folders of a certain domain, i.e. getting all `template`
folders or `site` folders

parameters.name: folder_ids
parameters.description: Required. Folder ids of the folders to be deleted

parameters.name: cascade_up
parameters.description:
Deleting folders will always cascade down. If a parent is deleted, then all
of its children are deleted. When cascade_up is true, if a child is
deleted, and it's parent no longer has any children, then it will be
deleted too. This will cascade all the way up the tree.

parameters.description:

for deleting folders within a specified domain

parameters.description:
Optional. Filter by field state. When not set, defaults to FIELD_STATE_UNSPECIFIED.

 - FIELD_STATE_UNSPECIFIED: Field state unspecified.
 - FIELD_STATE_ACTIVE: Field is active.
 - FIELD_STATE_DELETED: Field is deleted.
 - FIELD_STATE_ARCHIVED: Field is archived and no longer active, but not deleted. Cannot be applied to system fields.

 - SORT_FIELD_CREATED_AT: `SORT_FIELD_CREATED_AT` sorts fields by creation time.

get.description:
This endpoint is a streaming API that returns answers/responses from an inspection in real-time.

A stream API pushes data to the client when it becomes available and there is no need for the client to poll the requests to the server for new data.

Please note that if you're using a classic REST client, you may find that the data returned in the stream API are individual JSON structures that are not separated by `,` (comma) or encapsulated in a collection `[...]`.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description:
Number of messages to be returned in a single request. Maximum
100. Non-positive values are ignored.  The presence of `next_page_token` in
the response indicates that more results might be available. For example:
'20'

parameters.name: page_token
parameters.description:
If present, then retrieve the next batch of results from the
preceding call to this method.  `page_token` must be the value of
`next_page_token` from the previous response.  The values of other method
parameters should be identical to those in the previous call.
For example: 'MzA='

parameters.name: statuses
parameters.description:
Filter ScheduleItems to those with one of the the given status

 - STATUS_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified status value
 - ACTIVE: Default value - schedule item is still active
 - PAUSED: Schedule has been paused e.g. manually paused/org frozen
 - NO_TEMPLATE: The template used for this schedule item has been deleted/archived
 - NO_ASSIGNEE: No valid assignees exist for this schedule item anymore
 - FINISHED: The schedule item has hit its end date or count limit and is now complete
 - SUBSCRIPTION_INACTIVE: User/org subscription is inactive
 - NO_SITE: The site used for this schedule has been removed

requestBody.description: The schedule item to be created. The schedule will be created in the timezone of the authenticated user's profile timezone.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The id of the schedule item to update

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
Maximum number of schedule occurrence records to scan (1-1000).
• Default: 50 records
• Maximum: 1000 records
Please note that the number of records returned might be higher than the limit because a schedule occurrence might have multiple assignees.

parameters.name: show_active
parameters.description:
Include assignees from active schedules.
• true: Include assignees from active schedules
• false: Exclude assignees from active schedules
• default: true

parameters.name: show_finished
parameters.description:
Include assignees from finished schedules.
• true: Include assignees from finished schedules
• false: Exclude assignees from finished schedules
• default: true

parameters.name: show_paused
parameters.description:
Include assignees from paused schedules.
• true: Include assignees from paused schedules
• false: Exclude assignees from paused schedules
• default: true

parameters.name: template
parameters.description:
Filter assignees by template IDs used to create the schedules.
Provide template IDs to return assignees from schedules using those templates. Omit to return assignees from all templates. For example, ["template_xxx", "template_yyy"].

parameters.name: next_page_token
parameters.description:
Page token to retrieve next page. This token is returned in the metadata
of previous responses when more data is available.

PAGINATION USAGE:
- For the first page, omit `next_page_token` or provide an empty string.
- For subsequent pages, use `next_page_token` from previous response metadata.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
Maximum number of schedule occurrence records to scan (1-1000).
• Default: 50 records
• Maximum: 1000 records
Please note that the number of records returned might be higher than the limit because a schedule occurrence might have multiple assignees.

parameters.name: template
parameters.description:
Filter occurrences by template IDs used to create the schedules.
• Provide template IDs to only return occurrences from schedules using those templates. Omit to return occurrences from all templates. For example, ["template_xxx", "template_yyy"].

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description:
Return occurrences due on or after this timestamp.
• Use ISO 8601 format with timezone. For example, `2024-01-28T23:14:23.000Z`.
• Default: now - 5 months

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description:
Return occurrences due on or before this timestamp.
• Use ISO 8601 format with timezone For example, `2024-12-31T23:59:59.000Z`.
• Default: now + 1 month

parameters.name: next_page_token
parameters.description:
Page token to retrieve next page. This token is returned in the metadata
of previous responses when more data is available.

PAGINATION USAGE:
- For the first page, omit `next_page_token` or provide an empty string.
- For subsequent pages, use `next_page_token` from previous response metadata.

parameters.name: limit

parameters.description:
Page token to retrieve next page. This token is returned in the metadata
of previous responses when more data is available.

PAGINATION USAGE:
- For the first page: omit next_page_token (or provide empty string)
- For subsequent pages: use next_page_token from previous response metadata

post.description: Creates a new schedule with the specified configuration. For site/asset targets, sub-schedules are created asynchronously. Poll the schedule state to check creation progress.

requestBody.description:
CreateNewSchedule creates a schedule that generates recurring work items (e.g., inspections).

Schedules can target:
- Specific users (no target, just assignment.users)
- Sites (target.sites) - creates sub-schedules per site
- Assets (target.assets) - creates sub-schedules per asset

For site/asset targets, sub-schedules are created asynchronously.

delete.description: Deletes a schedule. Deleted schedules are no longer active and will not generate new occurrences. This action cannot be undone.

externalDocs.description: How to generate an API Token

@type.description:
A URL/resource name that uniquely identifies the type of the serialized
protocol buffer message. This string must contain at least
one "/" character. The last segment of the URL's path must represent
the fully qualified name of the type (as in
`path/google.protobuf.Duration`). The name should be in a canonical form
(e.g., leading "." is not accepted).

In practice, teams usually precompile into the binary all types that they
expect it to use in the context of Any. However, for URLs which use the
scheme `http`, `https`, or no scheme, one can optionally set up a type
server that maps type URLs to message definitions as follows:

* If no scheme is provided, `https` is assumed.
* An HTTP GET on the URL must yield a [google.protobuf.Type][]
  value in binary format, or produce an error.
* Applications are allowed to cache lookup results based on the
  URL, or have them precompiled into a binary to avoid any
  lookup. Therefore, binary compatibility needs to be preserved
  on changes to types. (Use versioned type names to manage
  breaking changes.)

Note: this functionality is not currently available in the official
protobuf release, and it is not used for type URLs beginning with
type.googleapis.com.

Schemes other than `http`, `https` (or the empty scheme) might be
used with implementation specific semantics.

year.description:
Year of the date. Must be from 1 to 9999, or 0 to specify a date without
a year.

month.description:
Month of a year. Must be from 1 to 12, or 0 to specify a year without a
month and day.

day.description:
Day of a month. Must be from 1 to 31 and valid for the year and month, or 0
to specify a year by itself or a year and month where the day isn't
significant.

google.type.Date.description:
* A full date, with non-zero year, month, and day values
* A month and day value, with a zero year, such as an anniversary
* A year on its own, with zero month and day values
* A year and month value, with a zero day, such as a credit card expiration
date

Related types are [google.type.TimeOfDay][google.type.TimeOfDay] and
`google.protobuf.Timestamp`.
google.type.Date.title:
Represents a whole or partial calendar date, such as a birthday. The time of
day and time zone are either specified elsewhere or are insignificant. The
date is relative to the Gregorian Calendar. This can represent one of the
following:

Allows for defining specific reasons for errors to display to customers.

 - BULK_DELETE_GROUPS_RESULT_CODE_UNSPECIFIED: Default
 - BULK_DELETE_GROUPS_RESULT_CODE_SUCCESS: Operation was a success
 - BULK_DELETE_GROUPS_RESULT_CODE_INTERNAL: Internal or unknown failure
 - BULK_DELETE_GROUPS_RESULT_CODE_MALFORMED_ID: Malformed group ID
 - BULK_DELETE_GROUPS_RESULT_CODE_NOT_FOUND: Group was not found

Describes the source of asset location.

 - ASSET_LOCATION_SOURCE_INSPECTION: Indicates that the source of asset location is inspection.
 - ASSET_LOCATION_SOURCE_GEO: Indicates that the source is GEO location.

Describes the sorting options for the list assets endpoint. By default,

s12.assets.v1.AssetsOrderBy.SortField.description:
Defaults to SORT_FIELD_CODE.

 - SORT_FIELD_UNSPECIFIED: The sort field is unspecified.
 - SORT_FIELD_CREATED_AT: Sort assets by `created_at`.
 - SORT_FIELD_MODIFIED_AT: Sort assets by `modified_at`.
 - SORT_FIELD_CODE: Sort assets by `code`.
 - SORT_FIELD_ASSET_FIELD: Sort assets by an asset-field.
 - SORT_FIELD_INSPECTED_AT: Sort assets by `inspected_at`.

s12.assets.v1.FieldValueType.description:
Describes each type of asset field.

 - FIELD_VALUE_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: Field value type unspecified.
 - FIELD_VALUE_TYPE_STRING: This type accepts plain text values.
 - FIELD_VALUE_TYPE_MONEY: This type accepts numerical values with currency codes. For example, "99.99" numerical value with "AUD" as the currency code.
 - FIELD_VALUE_TYPE_TIMESTAMP: This type accepts timestamp values in the Internet Date-Time format.
 - FIELD_VALUE_TYPE_SELECT: This type accepts select values.

units.description:
The whole units of the amount.
For example if `currencyCode` is `"USD"`, then 1 unit is one US dollar.

nanos.description:
Number of nano (10^-9) units of the amount.
The value must be between -999,999,999 and +999,999,999 inclusive.
If `units` is positive, `nanos` must be positive or zero.
If `units` is zero, `nanos` can be positive, zero, or negative.
If `units` is negative, `nanos` must be negative or zero.
For example £-1.75 is represented as `units`=-1 and `nanos`=-750,000,000.

s12.common.Status.description:
The `Status` type defines a logical error model and is based on the richer
error model as described in the guide - https://grpc.io/docs/guides/error/ A
`Status` message contains three pieces of data: error code, error message and
error details. The `Status.details` can be a collection of `Any` proto
message. Each proto message can describe an error type.

sub_premise.description:
A first-order entity below a named location
eg. Building, floor level, or suite

administrative_area_level_2.description: deprecated. For output, this will return the value of administrative_area_level for backward compatibility.

administrative_area_level_1.description:
A first-order civil entity below the country level. These do not exist in all countries.
eg. within the United States: state or province, within the UK: county, within Australia: state

country_codes.description:
Filter by specific country codes (ISO 3166-1 alpha-2).
Case-insensitive. Multiple values use OR logic (returns companies matching ANY code).
Example: ["US", "AU"] returns companies in US OR Australia.

administrative_areas.description:
Filter by specific administrative area names (administrative_area_level_1).
Case-insensitive. Multiple values use OR logic (returns companies matching ANY area).
Examples: US states ("CA", "NY"), Canadian provinces ("ON", "BC"),
UK counties, Australian states ("NSW", "VIC").
When combined with country_codes, uses AND logic
(e.g., country_codes=["US"] AND administrative_areas=["CA"] returns only US companies in California).

s12.contractors.v1.AddressDirectoryFilte.description:
Filter options for address directory queries.
All filtering is case-insensitive (e.g., "US", "us", "Us" all match "US").

expiring_soon_document_count.description:
The number of company documents that are expiring soon.
The service hard-codes "expiring soon" as documents that are expiring within the next 30 days.

pending_approval_document_count.description: The number of company documents that are pending approval.

s12.contractors.v1.ComplianceFilterType.description:
ComplianceFilterType specifies the type of document compliance issue to filter by.

 - COMPLIANCE_FILTER_TYPE_HAS_EXPIRED_DOCUMENTS: Filter for companies with expired documents.
 - COMPLIANCE_FILTER_TYPE_HAS_EXPIRING_SOON_DOCUMENTS: Filter for companies with documents expiring soon.
 - COMPLIANCE_FILTER_TYPE_HAS_PENDING_APPROVAL_DOCUMENTS: Filter for companies with documents pending approval.
 - COMPLIANCE_FILTER_TYPE_HAS_INCOMPLETE_REQUIREMENTS: Filter for companies with incomplete requirements.
 - COMPLIANCE_FILTER_TYPE_HAS_EXPIRED_USER_CREDENTIALS: Filter for companies with expired user credentials.
 - COMPLIANCE_FILTER_TYPE_HAS_EXPIRING_SOON_USER_CREDENTIALS: Filter for companies with user credentials expiring soon.
 - COMPLIANCE_FILTER_TYPE_HAS_PENDING_APPROVAL_USER_CREDENTIALS: Filter for companies with user credentials pending approval.

Deprecated. Will currently override compliance_filters if set.
Whether to include only companies that need attention due to non-compliance.

compliance_filters.description:
Optional. Filter by specific compliance issues (OR logic between selected types).
If empty, no compliance filtering is applied.
Companies will be returned if they match ANY of the selected compliance filter types.

address_filter.description:
Optional. Filter by address-related criteria (country and/or administrative area).
Filtering is case-insensitive. Multiple values within the same filter type use OR logic.
Country and administrative area filters combine with AND logic.

ids.description: Required. An array of folder IDs to move under the new parent.

Optional. New parent folder ID. If unspecified, the folder will be moved as
a root folder

domain.description:
domain represents the type/domain/hierarchy for the folder. This is used
for deleting folders within a specified domain. Defaults to site

new_parent_id.description:
New parent folder ID. Empty means the folder has been moved
as root.

folder_status.description:
Map of folders ids with their resulting status after removing the user
access to that folder. This map is populated with the list of folders
passed in through the `RemoveFoldersAssociation`
For example: <Key,Value> -> <5441fffcde344415bb7ccaceda27e196,
Status>

s12.directory.v1.RemoveFoldersAssociatio.description:
- UNKNOWN: Status unknown
 - REMOVED: Folder was successfully removed from the user
 - NOT_FOUND: Folder user association was not found
s12.directory.v1.RemoveFoldersAssociatio.title: Resulting status values after removing a folder from a user

user_status.description:
Map of UserIDs for users with an association type of "MEMBER" with their
resulting status after removing access to the folder. This map corresponds to the
list of UserIDs passed through `user_ids` in `RemoveUsersAssociationRequest`
For example: <Key,Value> -> <5441fffcde344415bb7ccaceda27e196, Status>

manager_status.description:
Map of UserIDs for users with an association type of "MANAGER" with their
resulting status after removing access to the folder. This map corresponds to the
list of UserIDs passed through `manager_user_ids` in `RemoveUsersAssociationRequest`
For example: <Key,Value> -> <5441fffcde344415bb7ccaceda27e196, Status>

s12.directory.v1.RemoveUsersAssociationR.description:
- UNKNOWN: Status unknown for user
 - REMOVED: User was successfully removed from the folder
 - NOT_FOUND: User folder association was not found
s12.directory.v1.RemoveUsersAssociationR.title: Resulting status values after removing a user from a folder

query.description:
Optional. The search value to query folders with when provided. When not
provided, either all of the organisations folders or users folders will be
returned in a paginated way

order_by.description: The order in which folders are sorted.

filters.description:
Optional. Filters to apply when getting folders. Used for only returning
folders that match the filter setting. i.e. only returning meta_label
'location'. Maximum of 1000 filters allowed.

only_assigned_folders.description:
When true, SearchFolders will only return folders that are assigned to
the user making the request. Defaults to false

only_leaf_nodes.description: When true, SearchFolders will only search location folders. Defaults to false.

include_members_count.description:
Whether to return the directly associated members
count with the folders. Defaults to false.

include_folders_count.description:
Whether to return the total number of folders discovered
in the query. Defaults to false

include_deleted_folders.description: Whether to return both deleted and non-deleted folders. Defaults to false

limit.description: The number of results to be returned. maximum 1500, default 500

page_token.description: The offset used for pagination. The starting number for the next query

ignore_folders_disabled.description:
Optional. When true, folders will still be returned when disabled for the
organisation

domain.description:
domain represents the type/domain/hierarchy for the folder. This is used
for getting folders of a certain domain, i.e. getting all `template`
folders or `site` folders

folders.description: An array of folders with their ancestors that match the search results

folder_count.description: The total number of folders found for the search query

next_page_token.description:
Optional. base64 encoded. If this field is present, the value should be
used in subsequent calls to SearchFolders to get the next page of results.
Otherwise, no further calls should be made.

folders_enabled.description: Whether or not the org settings setting is enabled.

s12.documents.v1.AcknowledgementType.description:
The acknowledgement type for a linked node — used both as the requirement on a link
and as the type recorded when a user acknowledges.
UNSPECIFIED means no acknowledgement is required.

 - ACKNOWLEDGEMENT_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: No acknowledgement required.

s12.documents.v1.BulkUpdateFileOwnersReq.description: Request to update and replace the existing owners of multiple files.

path.description:
An array of folder names that defines the path where the file will be stored.
The `path` is relative to the `parent_id` if it is provided. If not, it starts from the root folder.
The API will automatically create any folders in the path that don’t exist.
Each folder name must be 1-255 characters and exclude invalid characters.

name.description:
The name of the file.
Must be between 1-255 characters and exclude invalid characters.

file_type.description:
The type of the file to be created.
Please note that videos are currently not supported.

file_size.description:
The size of the file in bytes. Must not exceed 50MB.
This field is required if `file_type` is provided.

parent_id.description:
The ID of the folder where the file should be stored.
Any folders defined in the `path` will be created inside the parent folder.
If you don’t provide a parent_id, the file will be stored in the root folder.
To get the `parent_id`, open the folder in the SafetyCulture web app and copy the last part of the URL.
For example, in this link `https://app.safetyculture.com/documents/9dd76834-65ef-41d3-815c-964df32450e4`,
the parent ID is `9dd76834-65ef-41d3-815c-964df32450e4`.

s12.documents.v1.CreateFileRequest.title: Request to create a file in the specified folder

file_id.description: The ID of the file that was created.

upload_url.description:
The URL where you must upload the file’s binary content.
It is valid for 20 minutes.

path.description: If provided, the full folder path where the file was stored.

name.description: The name of the created file.

s12.documents.v1.DocumentsPublicService..description: Request to archive an item (file or folder).

target_folder_id.description:
The ID of the folder where the item (file or folder) should be moved.
If you don’t provide a target_folder_id, the item will be moved to the root folder.
To get the `target_folder_id`, open the target folder in the SafetyCulture web app and copy the last part of the URL.
For example, in this link `https://app.safetyculture.com/documents/9dd76834-65ef-41d3-815c-964df32450e4`,
the target folder ID is `9dd76834-65ef-41d3-815c-964df32450e4`.

file_type.description:
The type of the file to be created.
Please note that videos are currently not supported.

file_size.description: The size of the file in bytes. Must not exceed 50MB. This field is required if `file_type` is provided.

name.description: The new name of the file. Maximum of 255 characters and must exclude invalid characters.

valid_to.description: The expiry date of the file. Set year, month, and day to 0 to clear the file's expiry date.

search_term.description:
Search term to match against file or folder names.
This is case-insensitive, must be 1-130 characters, and exclude invalid characters.

item_ids.description: List of item IDs to filter results. Only items matching any of the IDs will be returned.

valid_to.description: TimeInterval to filter by expiry date. If only `from` is set, it will return files expiring after that date. If only `to` is set, it will return files expiring before that date. If both are set, it will return files expiring within that time range.

s12.loneworker.v1.JobStatus.description:
The enum representing the various states a job can be in during its lifecycle.

 - JOB_STATUS_ACTIVE_OK: The job is active and operating normally.
 - JOB_STATUS_OVERTIME: The job has exceeded the expected duration due to time expiry or missed check-in.
 - JOB_STATUS_IN_PANIC: The job is in a panic state.
 - JOB_STATUS_FINISHED: The job has finished.

s12.loneworker.v1.PanicReason.description:
The enum representing the various reasons a job can resolve a panic state.

 - PANIC_REASON_NO_PANIC: The job did not have a panic at all.
 - PANIC_REASON_FALSE_ALARM: The panic state was a false alarm.
 - PANIC_REASON_INCIDENT: The panic state was due to an incident.
 - PANIC_REASON_NEAR_MISS: The panic state was due to a near miss.

url.description:
The url has been deprecated for upload paths. For download paths, it is
still valid. The signed URL that can be used to upload or download the
media to the S12 system. UploadSignedURL is only valid for 5 minutes and
DownloadSignedURL for 20 minutes; this is from the first bytes received
i.e. if upload started within 5 minutes, it is still valid even if upload
takes 10 minutes to complete. It is safe to reject a signed URL and
re-request a new Upload/Download Signed URL.

s12.media.v1.Upload.title: Information to be included for upload in SignedURLResponse

upload_id.description:
This parameter is the upload id generated by S3 when initiating a new
multipart upload Used as an identifier of a multipart upload session

url.description: Destination url to upload the part

part_number.description: Number of the part in the multipart uploading sequence

s12.media.v1.Upload.MultipartUpload.Uplo.description:
This parameter returns the multi-part signed URL and part number
representing the position of a part in the upload sequence for AmazonS3
multi-part upload. It will return N number of UploadPartBody, each one is
used to upload one part of the whole file. An UploadPartBody will be
returned only if the file size is more than 10MB. Note that all parts
except the last one should be larger than 5MB or the upload will fail.
Each url is only valid for 10 minutes; this is from the first bytes
received i.e. if upload started within 10 minutes, it is still valid even
if upload takes more than 10 minutes to complete.

s12.permissions.v1.PermissionSetOrder.description:
The possible sort orders for permission sets.

 - PERMISSION_SET_ORDER_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified order.
 - PERMISSION_SET_ORDER_ASC: Ascending order.
 - PERMISSION_SET_ORDER_DESC: Descending order.

prioritise_seat_type.description: The seat type to prioritise. If set, permission sets with this seat type will be returned first. Cannot be used with criteria set to seat_type.

s12.permissions.v1.PermissionSetSort.description: The parameters to sort permission sets by (criteria and order).

s12.permissions.v1.PermissionSetSortCrit.description:
The possible criteria to sort permission sets by.

 - PERMISSION_SET_SORT_CRITERIA_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified sort.
 - PERMISSION_SET_SORT_CRITERIA_DEFAULT: Sort by the default order, which may or may not be equivalent as any other option.
 - PERMISSION_SET_SORT_CRITERIA_NAME: Sort by name.
 - PERMISSION_SET_SORT_CRITERIA_MIN_SEAT_TYPE: Sort by minimum seat type.
 - PERMISSION_SET_SORT_CRITERIA_LAST_MODIFIED: Sort by last modified date.

s12.permissions.v1.PermissionSetType.description:
The possible types of permission sets.

 - PERMISSION_SET_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified permission set type.
 - PERMISSION_SET_TYPE_DEFAULT: Default permission set.
 - PERMISSION_SET_TYPE_CUSTOM: Custom permission set.

id.description: The identifier of the updated schedule item

Unique identifier for this schedule-assignee relationship.
Format: "scheduleitem_{schedule_id}_assignee_{assignee_id}"
Example: "scheduleitem_123_assignee_456".

schedule_id.description:
ID of the schedule this assignee belongs to.
References a schedule from the FeedSchedules endpoint.

assignee_id.description:
ID of the individual or entity assigned to the schedule.
Could be a user ID, group ID (role), location ID (site), or location/group intersection.

type.description:
Type of entity assigned to this schedule.
• `user`: Individual user assigned
• `role`: Group-based assignment
• `location`: Location-based assignment (site)
• `location_intersection`: Group assignment based on location and role intersection.

organisation_id.description: ID of the organization that owns the assignment.

completion_rule.description: Completion requirement for the occurrence. One of: `any` (at least one assignee must complete) or `all` (every assignee must complete).

s12.scheduling.feed.v1.FeedScheduleAssig.description: A schedule assignee record returned from `FeedScheduleAssignees` V2
s12.scheduling.feed.v1.FeedScheduleAssig.title: FeedScheduleAssigneeRecord

data.description: The flat list of schedule assignee records.

id.description:
Unique identifier for this schedule occurrence.
Format: `{schedule_id}_{occurrence_id}_{assignee_id}`
Example: `schedule_123_occ_456_assignee_789`.

schedule_id.description:
ID of the schedule that generated this occurrence.
References a schedule from the FeedSchedules endpoint.

occurrence_id.description:
ID of the specific occurrence within the schedule's recurrence pattern.
Each occurrence represents one instance of the scheduled inspection.

template_id.description:
ID of the template used for inspections created from this occurrence.
References the inspection template that will be used.

start_time.description: When the occurrence becomes available for inspection (ISO 8601 timestamp format; for example, `2024-01-15T09:00:00Z`).

due_time.description: Final deadline (ISO 8601 timestamp format; for example, `2024-01-15T17:00:00Z`). After this time, the occurrence is missed and cannot be completed. For schedules with an overdue period, this comes after `miss_time`.

miss_time.description:
When this occurrence becomes overdue (ISO 8601 timestamp format).
• Present only if an overdue period configured; otherwise omitted.
• Between `miss_time` and `due_time`, unfinished occurrences are `OVERDUE`.
• It is `null` if an overdue period is not configured.

occurrence_status.description:
Current status of this scheduled occurrence.
• `TODO`: Not started.
• `IN_PROGRESS`: Inspection has been started but not completed.
• `COMPLETED`: Inspection has been finished on time.
• `LATE`: Inspection has been finished after the `miss_time` but before `due_time`.
• `OVERDUE`: Past `miss_time` but before `due_time`; not started.
• `MISSED`: Past `due_time` and has not been completed.
• `WONT_DO`: Intentionally skipped; `wont_do_note` is mandatory for this status.

audit_id.description:
ID of the inspection created from this occurrence.
• Present when the `occurrence_status` is `IN_PROGRESS` or `COMPLETED`.
• It is `null` for occurrences that haven't been started yet.
• Use this ID to fetch full inspection details from inspection APIs.

completed_at.description:
When this occurrence was marked as completed (ISO 8601 timestamp format).
• Present only when `occurrence_status` is `COMPLETED`.
• It is `null` for incomplete occurrences.

completion_rule.description: Completion requirement for the occurrence. One of: `any` (at least one assignee must complete) or `all` (every assignee must complete).

organisation_id.description: ID of the organization that owns the occurrence.

wont_do_note.description:
Reason provided when marking occurrence as `won't do`.
• Only populated when `occurrence_status` is `WONT_DO`.
• Contains the explanation for why the occurrence was skipped.

assignee_id.description:
ID of the specific entity assigned to complete this occurrence.
• It could be a user ID, role ID, or location ID, depending on assignment type.
• Use with FeedScheduleAssignees to get assignee details.

assignee_from.description:
Original assignment source that generated this individual occurrence.
• Format: `{type}_{id}` where type is user, group, role, or location.
• Examples: `user_xxx`, `role_yyy`, `location_zzz`
• Shows how this specific assignee was determined from the schedule's assignment rules.

s12.scheduling.feed.v1.FeedScheduleOccur.description: A schedule occurrence record returned from `FeedScheduleOccurrences` V2
s12.scheduling.feed.v1.FeedScheduleOccur.title: FeedScheduleOccurrence

data.description: The flat list of schedule occurrence records.

id.description:
Unique identifier for this schedule.
Format: `scheduleitem_{schedule_id}`
Example: `scheduleitem_123`.

title.description: Display name of the schedule. For example, `Weekly Safety Inspection` or `Monthly Equipment Check`.

recurrence.description:
Schedule recurrence pattern in RFC 5545 RRule format.
For example, `FREQ=DAILY`, `FREQ=WEEKLY;BYDAY=MO,WE,FR`, `FREQ=MONTHLY;BYMONTHDAY=1`
See: https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5545#section-3.3.10.

duration.description: Expected inspection duration (ISO 8601 duration format; for example, `PT30M` (30 minutes), `PT2H` (2 hours), `PT1H30M` (1.5 hours).

modified_at.description: Last updated timestamp (UTC format).

organisation_id.description: ID of the organization that owns this schedule.

created_at.description: Created timestamp (UTC format).

completion_rule.description: Completion requirement for the scheduled inspection. One of: `any` (at least one assignee must complete) or `all` (every assignee must complete).

status.description:
Current operational status of the schedule.
• `ACTIVE`: Schedule is running and creating occurrences.
• `PAUSED`: Schedule is temporarily stopped.
• `FINISHED`: Schedule has ended (past end date or max occurrences).

timezone.description: Timezone for schedule execution (IANA timezone identifier). For example, `America/New_York`, `Europe/London` or `UTC`.

timezone_type.description:
How the schedule's timezone is determined.
• `CUSTOM_TIMEZONE`: Uses the timezone specified in the 'timezone' field.
• `SITE_TIMEZONE`: Uses the timezone of the site where inspection occurs.

is_late_submit_allowed.description: Whether inspections can be started after the due date.

site_ids.description: Site IDs where the schedule is performed. May be empty for non-site-based schedules.

template_id.description: Template ID associated with the schedule.

created_by.description: ID of the user who created the schedule.

grace_period_duration.description: Grace period duration in ISO 8601 format. For example, `PT1H`.

ending_rule_end_date.description:
End date when ending_rule_type is `end_date` (ISO datetime string).
• Only populated when `ending_rule_type` = `end_date`.

ending_rule_end_after_repeats.description:
Number of repeats when `ending_rule_type` is `end_after_repeats`.
• Only populated when `ending_rule_type` = `end_after_repeats`.

intersections.description: Intersection group IDs (if schedule is using site+group intersection-based assignment).

asset_ids.description:
The assets assigned to this schedule item.
It is empty if asset IDs are not used for assignment.

s12.scheduling.feed.v1.FeedScheduleRecor.description: A schedule record returned from `FeedSchedules` V2
s12.scheduling.feed.v1.FeedScheduleRecor.title: FeedScheduleRecord

data.description: The flat list of schedule records.

s12.scheduling.v1.CompletionRule.description:
Completion rule enum.

 - COMPLETION_RULE_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified completion rule.
 - COMPLETION_RULE_ANY: Any assignee can complete the schedule.
 - COMPLETION_RULE_ALL: All assignees must complete the schedule.

work_type.description: The work to be done as part of the schedule.

recurrence.description: The recurrence pattern that defines when and how frequently the scheduled event occurs.

completion_rule.description:
Completion rule for the schedule.
Determines how many assigned users must complete the work for an occurrence to be considered complete.
Defaults to COMPLETION_RULE_ANY (any single assigned user can complete it) if not specified.

target.description:
Specifies which sites or assets this schedule applies to.
When set, a separate occurrence is created for each target (site or asset) at each recurrence.
When not set, the schedule creates occurrences directly for the assigned users.

assignment.description: Specifies which users are assigned to complete the work.

s12.scheduling.v1.CreateNewScheduleReque.description:
CreateNewSchedule creates a schedule that generates recurring work items (e.g., inspections).

Schedules can target:
- Specific users (no target, just assignment.users)
- Sites (target.sites) - creates sub-schedules per site
- Assets (target.assets) - creates sub-schedules per asset

For site/asset targets, sub-schedules are created asynchronously.

id.description: Unique identifier of the created schedule.

until_next_occurrence.description: Allow late submission until the next occurrence starts.

late_submission_duration.description:
Allow late submission for a specific duration after each occurrence ends.
Currently only supports a period of 14 days ("P14D"). ISO 8601 duration format.

s12.scheduling.v1.LateSubmissionRule.description: The conditions under which users can submit work late for each occurrence.

groups.description: Assign to members of a group.

site_members.description: Assign schedule to all members of the site(s) from the target.

site_members_in_group.description: Assign to users who are both site members AND in a specific group.

s12.scheduling.v1.ScheduleAssignmentRule.description:
ScheduleAssignmentRule defines who is assigned to complete the scheduled work.
Exactly one rule must be set.

Note: `site_members` and `site_members_in_group` require a target (sites or assets)
to be set, as they derive assignees from site membership.

s12.scheduling.v1.ScheduleByAssets.description:
ScheduleByAssets is a list of assets a schedule applies to.
Assignment and progress is tracked separately for each asset.
Assignees can be specified, or derived through the respective asset's site.

s12.scheduling.v1.ScheduleBySites.description:
ScheduleBySites is a list of sites a schedule applies to.
Assignment and progress is tracked separately for each site.
Assignees can be specified, or derived from site membership.

group_ids.description: Group IDs to assign. Currently supports only one group.

s12.scheduling.v1.ScheduleGroupAssignmen.description: ScheduleGroupAssignment assigns the scheduled work to all members of a group.

template_id.description: The ID of the inspection template to use.

s12.scheduling.v1.ScheduleInspectionWork.description: ScheduleInspectionWork configures the schedule to create inspections.

dtstart_rrule.title:
Supported RRULE frequencies and intervals:
- WEEKLY: BYDAY with multiple weekdays (e.g., BYDAY=MO,WE,FR)
- MONTHLY: BYMONTHDAY=1-28 OR BYSETPOS=1-4,-1 with BYDAY (single weekday)
- YEARLY: BYMONTH (required) with either BYMONTHDAY or BYSETPOS+BYDAY

duration.description: Duration in ISO 8601 format (e.g., "PT1H"). Defines the window from start to due.

use_site_timezone.description:
When true, use each target site's timezone instead of the RRULE timezone.
Only applicable when target is sites or assets (uses the asset's parent site timezone).
When enabled, any timezone specified in the RRULE will be ignored and set to the default time, UTC.

late_submission_rule.description:
The conditions under which users can submit work late for each occurrence.
When not set, late submission is not allowed.

s12.scheduling.v1.ScheduleRecurrence.description:
ScheduleRecurrence defines when and how often the schedule repeats.

RFC 5545 recurrence definition combining DTSTART and RRULE properties.
 DTSTART specifies the start time and timezone. RRULE defines the recurrence pattern.
 Timezone defaults to UTC if unspecified, or if `use_site_timezone` is true.

 Expected format: "DTSTART;TZID=America/New_York:20240101T140000\nRRULE:FREQ=DAILY;INTERVAL=1"

s12.scheduling.v1.ScheduleSiteMemberAssi.description:
ScheduleSiteMemberAssignment assigns work to all members of each target site.
For asset targets, assigns to members of the asset's parent site.
No additional configuration needed - membership is derived from the target.

group_ids.description: Group IDs for intersection filtering. Currently supports only one group.

s12.scheduling.v1.ScheduleSiteMemberInGr.description:
ScheduleSiteMemberInGroupAssignment assigns the scheduled work to users who are
both site members AND members of the specified group.
For asset targets, uses members of the asset's parent site.

s12.scheduling.v1.ScheduleTarget.description:
ScheduleTarget defines a group of entities a schedule applies to (e.g. sites, assets).
The schedule assigns and keeps track of progress for each entity.
Only one target can be set per schedule.

s12.scheduling.v1.ScheduleType.description:
- SCHEDULE_TYPE_UNSPECIFIED: Unspecified schedule type
 - SCHEDULE_TYPE_USERS: User-based schedule
 - SCHEDULE_TYPE_SITES: Site-based schedule
 - SCHEDULE_TYPE_ASSETS: Asset-based schedule
s12.scheduling.v1.ScheduleType.title: ScheduleType is the type of schedule

s12.scheduling.v1.ScheduleUserAssignment.description: ScheduleUserAssignment assigns the scheduled work to specific users.

s12.scheduling.v1.ScheduleWorkType.description:
ScheduleWorkType defines the type of work to be done as part of the schedule.
One is required.

completion_rule.description:
Completion rule for the schedule.
Determines how many assigned users must complete the work for an occurrence to be considered complete.

s12.scheduling.v1.UntilNextOccurrence.description:
UntilNextOccurrence indicates late submission is allowed until the next occurrence starts.
Used as a marker type in oneof fields.

The default value is in parts per million.

id.description: The id of the provisioned sensor.

name.description: The name of the reading, eg: "Relative Humidity".

type.description: The type of the reading, eg: "relative_humidity".

value.description: The value of the reading, eg: "90". The value is rounded to 2 decimal places.

unit.description:
The unit of the reading, eg: "Percentage". The unit might be converted from
the original unit to a corresponding standardised unit already supported on
SafetyCulture App for certain reading types.
For example, for the reading type "Odometer", if the unit is "Meters", it
will be converted to "Kilometers".

raw_value.description: The original value of the reading without any rounding.

raw_unit.description: The original unit of the reading without any conversion.

alias.description: Optional. A human-readable name for the reading specified by the user, e.g. 'Mileage', 'Usage'.

s12.sensors.v1.LatestSensorReading.description: LatestSensorReading represents the latest sensor reading.

page_token.description:
OPTIONAL. The value of next_page_token from a previous response. If
specified, the API will return the next batch of results following the
previous list sensors request.

page_size.description:
OPTIONAL. The number of sensors to return. By default, each request
returns 50 sensors. You can specify a page_size value between 1 to 100.

next_page_token.description: The value of next_page_token to be used in the next list sensors request.

value.description:
Value of the reading.
True if it is moving, false if it is not moving.

value.description: Value of the reading.

unit.description:

The default value is in kilometer.

s12.sensors.v1.OdometerReadingType.description: OdometerReadingType describes an odometer reading type.

 - ODOMETER_READING_TYPE_UNIT_KILOMETER: Kilometer unit.
 - ODOMETER_READING_TYPE_UNIT_MILE: Mile unit.
 - ODOMETER_READING_TYPE_UNIT_METER: Meter unit.

sensor_location_name.description: The location name for the sensor.

site_id.description: Optional. The SafetyCulture Site ID to associate the sensor with.

id.description:
The id of the provisioned sensor. This is the id that should be used to

value.description: Value of the reading.

unit.description:

The default value is in decibel.

ReceivedSignalStrengthIndicatorReadingType describes a received signal
strength indicator (RSSI) reading type.

value.description: Value of the reading.

Required. The ID of the status you're updating the action or issue to.
An issue can be in an "Open" or "Resolved" status, where each status is represented by hardcoded UUID values.
Issue statuses:
- Open: `547ed646-5e34-4732-bb54-a199d304368a`.
- Resolved: `450484b1-56cd-4784-9b49-a3cf97d0c0ad`.

An action can be in a "To do", "In progress", "Complete", or "Can't do" status, where each status is represented by hardcoded UUID values.
Action statuses:
- To do: `17e793a1-26a3-4ecd-99ca-f38ecc6eaa2e`.
- In progress: `20ce0cb1-387a-47d4-8c34-bc6fd3be0e27`.
- Complete: `7223d809-553e-4714-a038-62dc98f3fbf3`.
- Can't do: `06308884-41c2-4ee0-9da7-5676647d3d75`.

The title must be between 0 and 255 characters.

s12.tasks.v1.Category.description:
A category is used to classify an issue.
For example, you can filter issues by category for analytics, or give
users or groups access to issues from a category.

s12.tasks.v1.CategoryNotificationDetail.description:
CategoryNotificationDetail represents the different level of detail for category notifications.

 - CATEGORY_NOTIFICATION_DETAIL_UNSPECIFIED: CATEGORY_NOTIFICATION_DETAIL_UNSPECIFIED is the default value and should not be used.
 - CATEGORY_NOTIFICATION_DETAIL_PRIVATE: CATEGORY_NOTIFICATION_DETAIL_PRIVATE notifications will be restricted to public metadata.
 - CATEGORY_NOTIFICATION_DETAIL_SUMMARY: CATEGORY_NOTIFICATION_DETAIL_SUMMARY notifications will include public and private metadata.
 - CATEGORY_NOTIFICATION_DETAIL_FULL: CATEGORY_NOTIFICATION_DETAIL_FULL will include all metadata and detailed responses.

s12.tasks.v1.CategoryUserAccessAsReporte.title:
- CATEGORY_USER_ACCESS_AS_REPORTER_UNSPECIFIED: CATEGORY_USER_ACCESS_AS_REPORTER_UNSPECIFIED is the default value and should not be used
 - CATEGORY_USER_ACCESS_AS_REPORTER_ALWAYS: CATEGORY_USER_ACCESS_AS_REPORTER_ALWAYS the user will always gain access to issues they report
 - CATEGORY_USER_ACCESS_AS_REPORTER_NEVER: CATEGORY_USER_ACCESS_AS_REPORTER_NEVER the user will never gain access to issues they report
 - CATEGORY_USER_ACCESS_AS_REPORTER_WHEN_SITE_MEMBER: CATEGORY_USER_ACCESS_AS_REPORTER_WHEN_SITE_MEMBER the user will gain access to issues they report
when they have membership to the site that the issue has been raised at

users.description: The number of users that are the category is directly visible to

groups.description: The number of groups that the category is directly visible to

sites.description: The number of sites that the category is directly visible to

s12.tasks.v1.CategoryVisibilitySummary.title: CategoryVisibilitySummary represents a summary of the number of entities that the category is visible for

s12.tasks.v1.CollaboratorType.description:
- USER: The collaborator is an internal user.
 - GROUP: The collaborator is a group.
 - EXTERNAL_USER: The collaborator is an external user.
This happens when a task is assigned to an email address.
 - CONTRIBUTOR: The collaborator is a contributor.
A contributor is an individual who raises a task anonymously. For example,
creating an incident through the incident QR code functionality.

The collaborators involved in this issue.
Collaborators are users who have been assigned to the issue. The user will
appear in the "Assignee" field in the issue. Only one user can be assigned
to an issue at a time.

Optional. The date and time this issue was created.
If no date is provided, the current time will be used.
Must be sent in the Internet Date-Time format, more information about the type is
available here:
https://developers.google.com/protocol-buffers/docs/reference/google.protobuf#timestamp.

category_id.description:
Required. The ID of the issue's category.
Categories are configured at an organization level, and can be retrieved
from the `GetCategories` endpoint. The issue will not be created if the
category is missing, and an error will be returned.

Optional. ID of the issue's status.
If not set, this issue will be stored with the default status (Open).
An issue can be open or resolved, and these are represented by hardcoded
UUID values. Open status: `547ed646-5e34-4732-bb54-a199d304368a`. Resolved
status: `450484b1-56cd-4784-9b49-a3cf97d0c0ad`.

Site IDs can be retrieved on platform.

s12.tasks.v1.GetIncidentsRequest.Filter.description:
Filters represents a filters object which contains the filters available
when listing issues. You can provide an array of filters in the request
to get a more specific response. Each filter can have an `operator` and a
`value`. The `operators` are described in `FilterOperator`, and the `value`
will be a UUID associated with that filter type.

operator.description:
optional. Default to FilterOperator.IN and should only be
FilterOperator.IN

value.description: OR relation, represents the asset_id should be one of the value provided

operator.description: Optional, defaults to `IN` value.

value.description: The user ID of the assignee.

Will return issues where the user ID of the assignee matches the
provided value.

provided value.

to.description: optional. represents the time should be before the time provided

s12.tasks.v1.GetIncidentsRequest.Filter..description:
The options available when defining a filter. The only available option
is `IN`.

 - FILTER_OPERATOR_UNKNOWN: `FILTER_OPERATOR_UNKNOWN` represents the default value for a filter
operator, which is `IN`.
 - IN: `IN` returns results where the value is a direct match.

operator.description: Optional, defaults to `IN` value.

value.description: The ID of the priority.

s12.tasks.v1.GetIncidentsRequest.Filter..description:
Will return issues that have a matching priority.
The priority of an issue can be one of four options: none, low, medium
or high. Each of these are represented by a hardcoded UUID value.
No priority: `58941717-817f-4c7c-a6f6-5cd05e2bbfde`.
Low priority: `16ba4717-adc9-4d48-bf7c-044cfe0d2727`.
Medium priority: `ce87c58a-eeb2-4fde-9dc4-c6e85f1f4055`.
High priority: `02eb40c1-4f46-40c5-be16-d32941c96ec9`.

term.description: `term` represents the text to search for.

s12.tasks.v1.GetIncidentsRequest.Filter..description:
Will return issues where the title and/or description contain the search
term.

operator.description: Optional, defaults to `IN` value.

An issue can be open or resolved, and these are represented by
hardcoded UUID values.
Open status: `547ed646-5e34-4732-bb54-a199d304368a`.
Resolved status: `450484b1-56cd-4784-9b49-a3cf97d0c0ad`.

operator.description:
optional. Default to FilterOperator.IN and should only be
FilterOperator.IN

value.description:
OR relation, represents the task_id should be
one of the value provided

s12.tasks.v1.GetIncidentsRequest.SortDir.description:
The options available to define the sort direction.

 - SORT_DIRECTION_UNKNOWN: `SORT_DIRECTION_UNKNOWN` will return the default sort direction.
 - ASC: `ASC` will return the sorted issues in ascending order.
 - DESC: `DESC` will return the sorted issues in descending order.

s12.tasks.v1.GetIncidentsRequest.SortFie.description:
The options available to sort the issues by.
By default, the issues will be returned by the date they were last
modified in descending order. This should be used in combination with
`SortDirection` to determine how issues are sorted. If either
`SortField` or `SortDirection` is missing, the default sort order will be
used.

 - SORT_FIELD_UNKNOWN: `SORT_FIELD_UNKNOWN` will return the default sort order.
 - PRIORITY: `PRIORITY` will sort the issues by incident priority.
 - DATE_DUE: `DATE_DUE` will sort the issues by due date.
 - CREATED_AT: `CREATED_AT` will sort the issues by the date they were created.
 - MODIFIED_AT: `MODIFIED_AT` will sort the issues by the date they were last
modified.
 - OCCURRED_AT: `OCCURRED_AT` will sort the issues by the date they occurred at.

status_id.description:
Required. The ID of the status you're updating the action or issue to.
An issue can be in an "Open" or "Resolved" status, where each status is represented by hardcoded UUID values.
Issue statuses:
- Open: `547ed646-5e34-4732-bb54-a199d304368a`.
- Resolved: `450484b1-56cd-4784-9b49-a3cf97d0c0ad`.

An action can be in a "To do", "In progress", "Complete", or "Can't do" status, where each status is represented by hardcoded UUID values.
Action statuses:
- To do: `17e793a1-26a3-4ecd-99ca-f38ecc6eaa2e`.
- In progress: `20ce0cb1-387a-47d4-8c34-bc6fd3be0e27`.
- Complete: `7223d809-553e-4714-a038-62dc98f3fbf3`.
- Can't do: `06308884-41c2-4ee0-9da7-5676647d3d75`.

modified_at.description: Optional. The UTC time and date the modification took place.

title.description:
Required. The new title for the task.
The title must be between 0 and 255 characters.

modified_at.description: Optional. The timestamp of when this event occurred.

s12.tasks.v1.MetricType.description:
- TEMPERATURE: Temperature metric
 - HUMIDITY: Humidity metric
 - BATTERY: Battery level. Percentage based i.e. 0 - 100
 - SIGNAL: Signal strength metric
 - AIR_QUALITY_PM25: Air quality for particulate matter less than 2.5 microns
 - AIR_QUALITY_PM10: Air quality for particulate matter less than 10 microns
 - TEMPERATURE_2: Alternative temperature (Some hardware i.e. Avem Quirks do have 2 probes
and are sending 2 temperatures)
 - DIFFERENTIAL_AIR_PRESSURE: Differential air pressure
 - CO: Carbon monoxide
 - DOOR_OPEN_CLOSED: Door open/closed boolean metric
 - BATTERY_VOLTAGE: Battery voltage metric
 - WEATHER: Weather (e.g. sunny, thunderstorms)
 - WIND_SPEED: Wind speed
 - WIND_DIRECTION: Wind direction
 - WIND_GUST: Wind gust
 - CO2: Carbon dioxide
s12.tasks.v1.MetricType.title: Metric type represents a type of received metric

s12.tasks.v1.TimelineItem.Type.description:
The type of the timeline item.

 - TIMELINE_ITEM_UNKNOWN: Removed fields related to the cancelled actions/issues integration
An unknown type of timeline item.
 - TASK_CREATED: A timeline item for the creation of an action.
 - TASK_TITLE_UPDATED: A timeline item indicating that the title of the task has been updated.
 - TASK_DESCRIPTION_UPDATED: A timeline item indicating that the task's description has been updated.
 - TASK_ASSIGNEE_ADDED: A timeline item indicating that an assignee has been added to the task.
 - TASK_ASSIGNEE_REMOVED: A timeline item indicating that an assignee has been removed from the
task.
 - TASK_DUE_AT_UPDATED: A timeline item indicating that the due date of the task has been
updated.
 - TASK_STATUS_UPDATED: A timeline item indicating that the status of the task has been updated.
 - TASK_PRIORITY_UPDATED: A timeline item indicating that the priority of the task has been
updated.
 - TASK_COMMENT_ADDED: A timeline item indicating that a comment has been added to the task.
 - TASK_IMAGE_UPLOADED: A timeline item indicating that an image was added to the task.
This field is deprecated, and was replaced by the `TASK_MEDIA_ADDED`
type. It is kept here to maintain backwards compatibility.

@deprecated use TASK_MEDIA_UPLOADED instead
 - TASK_SITE_UPDATED: A timeline item indicating that the folder (site) of the task has been
updated.
 - TASK_DELETED: A timeline item indicating that this task has been deleted.
 - TASK_CATEGORY_UPDATED: A timeline item indicating that the category of this task has been
updated. This type is only relevant to issues.
 - INCIDENT_CREATED: A timeline item for the creation of an issue.
 - TASK_NOTIFICATION_SENT: A timeline item indicating that a notification about this task has been
sent.
 - TASK_MEDIA_UPLOADED: A timeline item indicating that media has been added to this task.
 - TASK_ASSIGNEE_UPDATED: reserved 17, 18, 19;
17 - 19 reserved for the cancelled actions/issues integration feature.
A timeline item indicating that the assignee of this task has been
updated.
 - TASK_LOCATION_ADDED: A timeline item indicating the geolocation of this issue.
This type is only relevant to issues.
 - TASK_INSPECTION_COMPLETED: A timeline item indicating that an inspection that has been linked to an
issue has been completed. This type is only relevant to issues.
 - TASK_INSPECTION_DELETED: A timeline item indicating that an inspection that has been linked to an
issue has been deleted. This type is only relevant to issues.
 - TASK_INSPECTION_ADDED: A timeline item indicating that an inspection has been linked to an
issue. This type is only relevant to issues.
 - TASK_ANSWER_ADDED: A timeline item indicating that an issue's custom question has been
answered. This type is only relevant to issues.
 - TASK_ASSET_UPDATED: A timeline item indicating that an action has an asset updated
 - TASK_LABELS_UPDATED: A timeline item indicating that an action has labels updated
 - TASK_SCHEDULE_UPDATED: A timeline item indicating that the schedule that created this action was
updated.
 - INCIDENT_CHILD_CREATED: A timeline item indicating that an action has been created from an issue
 - TASK_CUSTOM_FIELD_UPDATED: A timeline item indicating that a custom field on this task was updated.
 - TASK_OCCURRED_AT_UPDATED: A timeline item indicating that occurred at date for the task has been
updated.

is_attach_media_disabled.description:
Whether or not you can attach media to a template.
By default, media can be attached.
When disabled via this setting, media can still be attached via logic.

is_create_action_disabled.description:
Whether or not you can create actions to a template.
By default, actions can be created.
When disabled via this setting, actions can still be created via logic.

is_inspection_completion_dialog_confirma.description:
Whether or not the inspection completion dialog confirmation is disabled.
By default, the inspection completion dialog confirmation is enabled.
When enabled via this setting, the inspection completion dialog confirmation
will be closed automatically in two seconds after completing an inspection.

s12.typefield.v1.Domain.description:
The domain is used to specify which domain the data is coming from.
This allows the shared generic type field service to keep track of which domain owns the data,
and enforce any domain-specific access control rules.

 - DOMAIN_UNSPECIFIED: Domain Unspecified.
 - DOMAIN_GLOBAL: Owned and managed by system can be used by multiple domains
 - DOMAIN_ASSETS: Owned and created from Assets domain
 - DOMAIN_CONTRACTORS: Owned and created from Contractor Management domain
 - DOMAIN_LICENSES_AND_CREDENTIALS: Owned and created from the Licenses and Credentials domain
 - DOMAIN_TASKS: Owned and created from the Tasks domain
 - DOMAIN_USERS: Owned and created from the Users domain
 - DOMAIN_INVESTIGATIONS: Owned and created from the Investigations domain
 - DOMAIN_STRUCTURES: Owned and created from the Organisational Structures domain
 - DOMAIN_INTEGRATIONS_HRIS_STAGING: Owned and created from the Integrations domain

Optional. If present, then retrieve the next batch of results from the
preceding call to this method. `page_token` must be the value of
`next_page_token` from the previous response.  The values of other method
parameters should be identical to those in the previous call.
For example: 'ODFBMzQ3MDYtNzQxNy00RDZGLThDNjE1MEFDMkM4MTQ3NDQ='.

s12.userdocuments.v1.DocumentVersionFilt.description: DocumentVersionFilter is used to filter document versions based on various criteria.
ApprovalStatusFilter is the approval status of the document.
CompaniesFilter is used to query for documents based on the companies the associated user is assigned to.

s12.userdocuments.v1.DocumentVersionFilt.description: DocumentTypeID is used to query for documents of a particular type.
ExpiryStatusFilter is the expiry status of the document.
GroupsFilter is used to query for documents based on the groups they are assigned to.

The total count of media items associated with this credential version.
This count is available regardless of whether the user has permissions to view the media.

s12.userdocuments.v1.DocumentVersionMeta.description: Metadata associated with a credential version.

s12.userdocuments.v1.DocumentVersionSort.description:
DocumentVersionSortField is used to sort document versions based on various criteria.

 - DOCUMENT_VERSION_SORT_FIELD_EXPIRY: `DOCUMENT_VERSION_SORT_FIELD_EXPIRY` will sort credentials by expiry date.
 - DOCUMENT_VERSION_SORT_FIELD_ISSUE_DATE: `DOCUMENT_VERSION_SORT_FIELD_ISSUE_DATE` will sort credentials by issue date.
 - DOCUMENT_VERSION_SORT_FIELD_DOCUMENT_TYPE_NAME: `DOCUMENT_VERSION_SORT_FIELD_DOCUMENT_TYPE_NAME` will sort credentials by document type name.
 - DOCUMENT_VERSION_SORT_FIELD_MODIFIED_AT: `DOCUMENT_VERSION_SORT_FIELD_MODIFIED_AT` will sort credentials by modified at date.
 - DOCUMENT_VERSION_SORT_FIELD_USER_NAME: `DOCUMENT_VERSION_SORT_FIELD_USER_NAME` will sort credentials by user name.
 - DOCUMENT_VERSION_SORT_FIELD_APPROVAL_STATUS: `DOCUMENT_VERSION_SORT_FIELD_APPROVAL_STATUS` will sort credentials by approval status.

s12.userdocuments.v1.ExpiryStatus.description:
@deprecated. Use s12.common.ExpiryStatus instead.
The status of a credential based on its expiration period.

document_versions.description: The credential versions for this particular credential.

page_size.description: The number of credentials to return in a single request. This defaults to 20.

next_page_token.description:
If there are more results available, this will be set. If this is unset, there are no more results.
To get the next set of results, call `GetDocuments` again with `page_token` set to this value.

next_page_token.description:
If there might be more results than those appearing in this response, then
`next_page_token` is included. If empty then there are no more results.
To get the next set of results, call this method again using the value of
`next_page_token` as `page_token`.

s12.users.v1.CreateOrUpdateResponseUser..description:
- LEVEL_INFO: Indicates an error is only for informational purposes and can be safely ignored.

Ex: An ID was changed in formatting from S12ID to UUID (no functional change, just an FYI)
 - LEVEL_WARNING: Indicates an error can be ignored, but may have a different or unexpected outcome than requested.

Ex: Adding a user-site association for both parent and child sites (will only be added to the parent, but no
user intervention is needed)
 - LEVEL_ERROR: Indicates an error where there was no way to auto-resolve the issue, but processing of the remaining data was
able to continue

Ex: A requested group does not exists in the organisation (stop processing this group, move on to the next)
 - LEVEL_FATAL: Indicates an error that caused the process to catastrophically fail and further data processing is not possible

Ex: Organisation has a negative number of seats used (we cannot recover from this 'impossible' state)

s12.users.v1.CreateOrUpdateResponseUser..title:
- REASON_UNSPECIFIED: Default failure value.
Avoid using in favor of defining a specific failure case.
 - REASON_INTERNAL: Something went wrong but, we're not quite sure.
Avoid using in favor of defining a specific failure case.
 - REASON_USER_EXISTS: User already exists with this username.
 - REASON_USER_NOT_FOUND: User ID was unable to be found.
 - REASON_DUPLICATE_ROW: Duplicate rows detected.
 - REASON_DUPLICATE_USERNAME: Duplicate username detected - Different to REASON_DUPLICATE_ROW, this is thrown if the user manages to get a username past our validation, but our internal system detects a duplicate.
 - REASON_MUST_HAVE_ID_OR_USERNAME: A record must have an ID or a username to support with updating.
 - REASON_INVALID_ID_FORMAT: ID is in an invalid format
 - REASON_INVALID_USERNAME_FORMAT: Username is in an invalid format
 - REASON_ID_USERNAME_MISMATCH: Username does not match existing username, username updates are currently not supported.
 - REASON_FIELD_MANDATORY_FOR_CREATION: The field referenced is mandatory for creation.
 - REASON_CANNOT_CREATE_DEACTIVATED_USERS: Cannot create deactivated users
 - REASON_FIELD_CHANGE_NOT_ALLOWED_FOR_CALLER: Caller does not have permission to change this field for this action type.
 - REASON_INVALID_VALUE_GENERIC: Generic invalid field value error.
Avoid using in favor of defining a specific failure case.
 - REASON_INVALID_SEAT_PERMISSION_SET_COMBINATION: Invalid permission set/seat assignment.
 - REASON_INVALID_VALUE_PASSWORD_TOO_WEAK: Password does not match password requirements
 - REASON_INVALID_VALUE_PASSWORD_CANNOT_UPDATE_USERS_PASSWORD: Updating an existing users password is not supported
 - REASON_INVALID_VALUE_REFERENCE_NAME_DOES_NOT_FOUND: Invalid site, group, or attribute Name reference.
 - REASON_INVALID_VALUE_REFERENCE_ID_DOES_NOT_FOUND: Invalid site, group, or attribute ID reference.
 - REASON_PERMISSION_SET_PERMISSION_DENIED: Requester does not have the permission(s) needed to interact with Permission Sets for other users
 - REASON_PERMISSION_SET_USER_NOT_ACTIVE: The requested user is not active and therefore cannot have Permission Set interactions
 - REASON_PERMISSION_SET_NOT_FOUND: The requested Permission Set cannot be found in the organisation
 - REASON_INVALID_PERMISSION_SET_FOR_ORGANISATION_TIER: The Permission Set is not allowed to be associated with this type of organisation.
Ex: Trying to use a custom Permission Set in a Free organisation (which is only allowed in Paid orgs)
 - REASON_USER_ATTRIBUTE_DUPLICATE_ATTRIBUTE: User attribute has already been declared in the input for this user
 - REASON_USER_ATTRIBUTE_NOT_FOUND: User attribute could not be found in the user's organisation
 - REASON_USER_ATTRIBUTE_INVALID_ATTRIBUTE_TYPE: User attribute type is not supported.
This may indicate a new field type has been added, but the service has not been updated to work out how to sanitise it
 - REASON_USER_ATTRIBUTE_INVALID_SELECT_VALUE: Provided value is not one of the values allowed by this Select field
 - REASON_USER_ATTRIBUTE_INVALID_USER_ID_VALUE: Provided value cannot be coerced to a User ID format
 - REASON_USER_ATTRIBUTE_INVALID_TIMESTAMP_VALUE: Provided value cannot be coerced to a Timestamp format
 - REASON_USER_ATTRIBUTE_INVALID_STRING_VALUE: Provided String value is not allowed.
Ex: a non-nil, empty string
 - REASON_INVALID_SEAT_TYPE_FOR_ORGANISATION_TIER: Invalid seat type for the organisation tier, i.e. using lite seats on a free team.
 - REASON_SEAT_LIMIT_REACHED: The request exceeds the seat limit
 - REASON_GROUP_NOT_FOUND: Group is not found, or does not exist in the current organisation
 - REASON_GROUP_DUPLICATE_ENTRY: Group has already been declared in the input for this user
 - REASON_GROUP_CANNOT_ASSIGN_TO_DEACTIVATED_USER: Group is not allowed to be assigned to a user that is - or will be - deactivated
 - REASON_GROUP_INVALID_REMOVE_REFERENCE: Group remove reference is invalid
 - REASON_GROUP_ASSOCIATION_LIMIT_REACHED: User cannot be assigned to more then 100 groups
 - REASON_GROUP_REFERENCE_AMBIGUOUS: Group reference is ambiguous, i.e. multiple groups with the same name
 - REASON_SITE_NOT_FOUND: Site is not found, or does not exist in the current organisation
 - REASON_SITE_DUPLICATE_ENTRY: Site has already been declared in the input for this user
 - REASON_SITE_CANNOT_ASSIGN_TO_DEACTIVATED_USER: Site is not allowed to be assigned to a user that is - or will be - deactivated
 - REASON_USER_ALREADY_ASSIGNED_TO_ANCESTOR_SITE: User is already assigned to a site further up the hierarchy (ex: requesting Site, but user is already in ancestor Country)
 - REASON_SITE_INVALID_REMOVE_REFERENCE: Site remove reference is invalid
 - REASON_SITE_ASSOCIATION_LIMIT_REACHED: User is already associated with the maximum number of sites
 - REASON_SITE_REFERENCE_AMBIGUOUS: Site reference is ambiguous, i.e. multiple sites with the same name
 - REASON_USER_ATTRIBUTE_DISABLED: User attribute is disabled
 - REASON_LAST_ADMINISTRATOR: Changing this permission set, or status will remove the last admin for the org
 - REASON_FIELD_VALUE_INVALID_MIN_LENGTH: Field value does not meet the minimum length
 - REASON_FIELD_VALUE_INVALID_MAX_LENGTH: Field value does exceeds the maximum length
 - REASON_COMPANY_NOT_FOUND: Company is not found, or does not exist in the current organisation
 - REASON_COMPANY_DUPLICATE_ENTRY: Company has already been declared in the input for this user
 - REASON_COMPANY_CANNOT_ASSIGN_TO_DEACTIVATED_USER: Company is not allowed to be assigned to a user that is - or will be - deactivated
 - REASON_COMPANY_INVALID_REMOVE_REFERENCE: Company remove reference is invalid
 - REASON_COMPANY_MEMBERSHIP_CHANGE_NOT_ALLOWED_FOR_CALLER: Caller does not have permissions to modify user membership for this company
 - REASON_GROUP_MEMBERSHIP_CHANGE_NOT_ALLOWED_FOR_DYNAMIC_GROUP: Group membership cannot be manually assigned to dynamic groups with automated membership rules
 - REASON_SITE_MEMBERSHIP_CHANGE_NOT_ALLOWED_FOR_DYNAMIC_SITE: Site membership cannot be manually assigned to dynamic sites with automated membership rules

s12.users.v1.CreateOrUpdateResponseUser..description:
PENDING --> ERRORED : Blocking errors (e.g. invalid username)
PENDING --> VALIDATED : No errors
PENDING --> VALIDATED_WITH_ERRORS : Non blocking errors

VALIDATED --> ERRORED : Blocking errors (e.g. create failed)
VALIDATED --> COMPLETED : No errors
VALIDATED --> COMPLETED_WITH_ERRORS : Non blocking errors

VALIDATED_WITH_ERRORS --> ERRORED : Blocking errors
VALIDATED_WITH_ERRORS --> COMPLETED_WITH_ERRORS : Non blocking errors
```

 - STATUS_PENDING: User pending validation.
 - STATUS_ERRORED: Critical blocking error.
 - STATUS_VALIDATED: User was successfully validated with no errors. Now pending create or update.
 - STATUS_VALIDATED_WITH_ERRORS: User was validated, but there was non-blocking errors. Now pending create or update.
 - STATUS_COMPLETED: User was created or updated with no errors.
 - STATUS_COMPLETED_WITH_ERRORS: User was created or updated with non blocking errors.

used.description:
The number of seats used for each type, up to the available limit
If you have 10 seats available, and the request wants to use 11 the value will be 10.

required.description: The additional number of seats required for the request to be fully successful.

password.description: Password for the user. This field is used only during user creation.

seat_type.description: User's seat type: full, lite, or guest.

timezone.description: Timezone, must be a valid timezone in the IANA time zone database.

language.description: Language preference for the user. Follows the ISO-639 code. For example, `en-US`.

permission_set.description: The permission set assigned to the user. Must be a valid name or ID.

groups.description: Groups the user is assigned to.

sites.description: Sites the user is assigned to.

companies.description: Contractor companies the user is assigned to.

email_settings.description: Controls whether a welcome email is sent to newly created users. By default, an email is sent if this field is not set. Overrides any request-level `EmailSettings` with this user-specific setting.

reference.description: Attribute reference. Must be a valid Name or ID for the user field.

valid.description: Indicates whether the value is valid and will be applied to the user.

string_value.description: The string value of a field.

timestamp_value.description: The timestamp value of a field.

uuid_value.description: The UUID value of a field.

remove.description: References from which the user will be removed. ONLY supports "*" to remove from all existing references.

add.description: References to assign the user to. Each must be a valid, case-sensitive name or ID for the resource.

valid.description: Read-only. Indicates whether the reference is valid and will be applied to the user.

hierarchy.description:
map of the hierarchy of the reference, such as a group or site.
The target resource is the last element in the hierarchy.

s12.users.v1.CreateOrUpdateUser.Referenc.description:
A reference to another resource, such as a group, site, or permission set.
At least one of `id` or `name` is required.
If both are provided, the `id` is used and `name` is ignored.
If only `name` is provided, it resolves the corresponding `id`.

s12.users.v1.CreateOrUpdateUsersJob.Stat.description:
PENDING --> ERRORED : Blocking errors (e.g. putting the job on the queue errored)
PENDING --> VALIDATING
PENDING --> CANCELLED

VALIDATING --> VALIDATED : No errors
VALIDATING --> VALIDATED_WITH_ERRORS : Non blocking errors
VALIDATING --> ERRORED : Blocking errors (e.g. putting the job on the queue errored)
VALIDATING --> EXECUTING
VALIDATING --> CANCELLED

EXECUTING --> COMPLETED : No errors
EXECUTING --> COMPLETED_WITH_ERRORS : Non blocking errors
EXECUTING --> ERRORED : Blocking errors
EXECUTING --> CANCELLED
```

 - STATUS_PENDING: Job pending validation.
 - STATUS_ERRORED: Job has critical blocking error.
 - STATUS_VALIDATING: Job is currently validating.
 - STATUS_VALIDATED: Job was successfully validated with no errors. Now pending create or update.
 - STATUS_VALIDATED_WITH_ERRORS: Job was validated, but there was non-blocking errors. Now pending create or update.
 - STATUS_EXECUTING: Job is currently creating or updating.
 - STATUS_COMPLETED: Job created or updated with no errors.
 - STATUS_COMPLETED_WITH_ERRORS: Job created or updated with non blocking errors.
 - STATUS_CANCELLED: Job was cancelled.
